TextENGNew: "Well then, it's lunchtime, everyone!!"

changeReason: Sound transliteration changed: JPN みー is rendered as 'Meep' in original but 'Mii' in new, which is a direct transliteration.

TextENGNew: "Ahahaha!@k@vS03/02/140200139. It's been so long since all five of us ate together!@k@vS03/02/140200140. I'm so happy!"
"Couldn't agree more.@k@vS03/03/140300266. Come on, you too, Kei-chan!@k@vS03/03/140300267. Bring your desk over here!"
Satoko was beaming as she cheerfully pushed her desk over to join ours.
...Even knowing that nothing had actually been resolved... it was heartwarming to see this exchange that recalled the happy days we'd shared.
"...I can't keep making Rika prepare my lunches forever... I feel bad."
"...It's fine.@k@vS03/05/140500049. ...Making Satoko's bento is one of my little joys, you know."
Rika-chan really was a great person.@kHaving a best friend like her must be incredibly reassuring...
``...I put the big hamburger steak in my lunch,@k@vS03/05/140500051. and gave Satoko the burnt one.``
changeReason: JPN says ハンバーグ (hamburger steak) but original translates as 'steak'; new correctly says 'hamburger steak.'

TextENGNew: "That way only Satoko gets the sad one, and nipah~"
I take it back.@k...Well, hmm.@kShe's Rika-chan, all right. Yeah.
"Hey, look, look!@k@vS03/02/140200142. Today, see, it's omurice~!@k@vS03/02/140200143. The mushrooms and stuff look delicious, right? Right? Hau!"
"Splendid handiwork, as always.@k@vS03/04/140400107. You'll make someone a wonderful bride someday!"
``A b-b-b-b-bride, a bride~~@k@vS03/02/140200145_1. A bride~ is what I'll be~~@k@vS03/02/140200145_2. I wanna take it hooome~~``
changeReason: JPN お嫁を食べると means 'eating a bride' which the original captures; new translation changes to 'A bride is what I'll be, I wanna take it home' -- different meaning.

TextENGNew: "Talking about brides is an off-limits word for Rena.@k@vS03/03/140300269. Let's refrain from now on~."
"...I shall reflect upon my actions. A tiny bit."
"By the way, what's in your lunch, Mion?@k@vS03/01/140100511. Today's looks different—I'm intrigued."
"Just a cozy assortment of last night's leftovers."
"The simmered dishes turned out really well.@k@vS03/03/140300272. Look, the color of the daikon is pretty good, right?"
"...Wow~ that looks delicious."
"Simmered dishes are at their best after sitting overnight!@k@vS03/01/140100513. This looks like it'd be amazing! Let me try some!"
"Wh-wh-what did you just say?!?!@k@vS03/04/140400110. Hand over your portion as well, then!@k@vS03/04/140400111. Here I come!"
Elbow!@|@y Fist!@|@y Headbutt!@|@y She's even using her knees—raaagh!!
"Oh-ho-ho!!@k@vS03/04/140400112. At close quarters, the advantage is mine!@k@vS03/04/140400113. Ohohohoho!"
"Hmm, my, my—Keiichi-san's daikon is absolutely delicious!"
"...Damn it... Closing the distance right at the start and chaining an instant combo...@k@vS03/01/140100516. ...I'm the one who declared the attack and I still lost..."
This kind of exchange... it had been so long.
...It seemed they'd ended up going with 'wait and see' after all, but... maybe the uncle would reflect on going too far, and maybe things would improve from now on...
...If her treatment improved even a little... would that be enough...?
Satoko was grinning wider than I'd ever seen as she happily stuffed her cheeks with the daikon she'd seized from me.
She was so endearing like that...@kand just as I'd done so many times before, I reached out to ruffle her hair, to give that little head of hers a good tousle—
Smack...!
Clatter... clinkety-clank...
"......Satoko...chan...?"
...It happened so suddenly... that for a moment, I couldn't process what had just occurred.
The back of my hand... was stinging.
...Wait... I reached out to pet Satoko's head... and she... slapped my arm away...?
"...W-... What's wrong, Satoko?"
Satoko was... furiously scratching at her own head... as if a sudden rash had erupted and driven her mad with itching.
...It was so sudden... so completely out of nowhere... that I couldn't make sense of it.
"...W-What's wrong, Satoko?"
At some point... a thin sheen of sweat had broken out over her entire body... and her breathing had gone ragged.
When I... reached for her head, she recoiled... was that what happened...?
"...Hey... did you always... dislike having your head petted...?"
Once more... very slowly... I extended my palm... and moved it toward Satoko's head.
...The instant my hand made contact with her head—
...a shock like a bolt of electricity ripped through her, and Satoko erupted—
Crash, SLAM!!!
She seized my arm with both hands... and hurled it away as if flinging off a snake that had coiled around her... as if casting aside something filthy—
...It happened too fast for me to resist.@kI toppled backwards, chair and all.
The entire classroom... went deathly silent.
Satoko let out a sound like a dying animal's last cry... swaying and staggering... backing away from me.
"Ugh... ghhhh... hhhgh...!!!"
Then... she threw up everything she had just eaten.
The change in her was so extreme that none of us could believe what we were seeing.
"...A-... Are you okay, Satoko...? Are you feeling si—"
"...Eeeeeeeeeee-aaaaaaaaaaahhhhh!!!"
I reached out a hand—and she shoved me away with all her might.
The force was incredible. I hit the floor again.
Satoko shielded her head with both hands, growling, and stumbled back several steps.
...Then she began swinging her arms wildly in every direction.@k...Students sitting nearby scrambled to get away.
"Stop it, Satoko!!@k@vS03/03/140300275. W-What's gotten into you?!"
"Eeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee...!!!"
Mion's words seemed to stab at her like blades—Satoko screamed as if in physical agony.
...No one... could bring themselves to speak to her.
All the students had evacuated to our side... leaving Satoko utterly alone in one corner of the classroom.
Around her... overturned desks and chairs, scattered lunches... the scene was a disaster.
...The only sounds were Satoko's harsh, ragged breathing... and the drone of the cicadas.
As the wire-taut tension in Satoko finally loosened...@kshe seemed to realize what she had done, and terror gripped her. Trembling violently... she sank to the floor.
...But it was impossible to tell who her apology was directed at.
...So... I decided to answer on everyone's behalf, for the time being.
"...........N-No, it's okay... Are... you all right...?"
"...I... I-I... I'm sorry, I'm sorry... I'm sorry, I'm sorry... hiiii... hiii... aaahhh... ahhh..."
Our words weren't connecting at all.
...Satoko clutched her own shoulders... and edged backward, backward...
Bang!
...She backed into the cleaning supply locker.@kEven that made her let out a short scream.
...The impact knocked the bucket off the top of the locker, and when it hit the floor with a tremendous clang... it terrified Satoko beyond anything else.
She nearly jumped out of her skin and threw her arms around the bundle of curtains.
...No one... could speak to her.@k...They'd forgotten even to blink... all they could do was stare.
"...Ooh... ahh... aaahh...@k@vS03/04/140400121. I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry..."
...Satoko... you... what on earth...
I got to my feet... stepped out of the group... and walked toward Satoko.
"...What...... happened to you..."
"I told you, I told you...!!!@k@vS03/04/140400124. I don't know anything! I don't know anything!!@k@vS03/04/140400125. I was good, I was a good girl!! I didn't do anything wrong!!"
changeReason: JPN says 違うから (you're wrong/that's not right) and 許して (forgive me); new changes to 'I was good, I was a good girl! I didn't do anything wrong!' which alters the content.

TextENGNew: "C-Calm down...@k@vS03/01/140100525. ...Satoko... do you know who I am...?"
"I don't want this anymore, I don't want this anymore... I hate it, I hate it... hiiii... hiiiii!!!"
Every step I took closer, she clutched the curtains tighter... and wailed louder.
"...Satoko... it's me... Keiichi.@k@vS03/01/140100527. Don't you recognize me?!@k@vS03/01/140100528. I would never... hurt you!!"
"Nooooooooooooooooo!!!@k@vS03/04/140400128. Waaaaaaahhhhhh!!!"
"Just calm down...!@k@vS03/01/140100530. It's all right... I'm on your side...!!"
"Help... help meeeee!!!@k@vS03/04/140400131. I don't want to be scared... it hurts... I don't want... I don't want...... waaaaaahhhhhh!!!"
Someone suddenly grabbed my shoulder and yanked me back.
...It was Rena.
"...Sorry, Keiichi-kun!@k@vS03/02/140200149. Step back for a bit!!"
Taking my place, Rena rushed to Satoko's side...
"...It's okay, Satoko-chan, there's nothing to be afraid of anymore...!@k@vS03/02/140200151. It's Rena! It's just Rena...!"
"No, no, no... no more, no more... not that... I don't want... wahhhhh, waaahhh...!!!@k@vS03/04/140400134. Nii-nii... Nii-nii..."
"Help me...! Help meeeee!!@k@vS03/04/140400135. Nii-nii, save me... Nii-nii, Nii-nii...@k@vS03/04/140400136. Nii-nii... Nii-nii..."
...Clinging to the curtains... calling out for Nii-nii over and over... Satoko begged for forgiveness from something, begged for rescue from something...
Mion... was grinding her teeth, clenching her jaw... tears spilling from her tightly shut eyes.

``......What the hell... is going on...@k@vS03/01/140100532. ...Wasn't it... wasn't it all supposed to end yesterday...?@k@vS03/01/140100533. Weren't we... supposed to go back... to our normal lives from today...?!@k@vS03/01/140100534. Mion... Mioonnnnnn!!``
CRASH!!@k...There was a tremendous noise.
...It was the sound of Rena... kicking the cleaning supply locker over.
Everyone was startled... by Rena's unexpected act of violence.
...Then Rena... wrapped her arms around the sobbing Satoko from behind........................ and, stifling her own cries, wept alongside her.
"...Rena... what's wrong..."
"...............Sorry.@k@vS03/02/140200153. ...Keiichi-kun... just be quiet for a minute...@k@vS03/02/140200154. ...Please, just shut up for a minute..."
"...What was that about?@k@vS03/01/140100537. ...What's going on...?!@k@vS03/01/140100538. Hey, Rena, what just—"
"I said shut up!!@k@vS03/02/140200155. I told you to be quiet!!@k@vS03/02/140200156. ...Be quiet...!!"
Rena's raw, undisguised hostility hit me like a physical blow.
...For Rena, it was nothing more than a momentary outburst of emotion... but for me, it carried something deeper.
...Behind Rena's fury... I could feel her heart... reaching me.
"No, no, no, no... no more, no more, no more... wahhh, waahh..."
"Forgive us... we couldn't do anything for you... forgive us..."
"Wah... waaaaahhhhhhhh!!@k@vS03/04/140400139. Nii-nii...!@k@vS03/04/140400140. Nii-nii..."
Forgive us... for being unable... to do anything.
Rena's words... sent a sensation through me like the blood vessels in the back of my skull were crumbling to ash.
...My vision receded... blurred with gray... and the world lost all its color.
...I'd believed... that Satoko could be saved.
...And while I knew the sooner the better... I'd thought there was no real deadline, no time limit.@k...That we still had time... was what I'd assumed.
I... had been making a terrible mistake.
...I'd thought Satoko could be hurt... but never that she could break.
...What a catastrophic miscalculation!!
People... break.@k...To assume they won't... is absolutely insane!!
We... needed to save Satoko.
...We needed to save her before she broke.
...That fatal failure of recognition... had led to this day.
Whose fault was it?
...That went without saying.@k...Mine—no, ours.@k...We who bore the obligation to save Satoko had allowed time to be wasted like this.
If I accepted that premise... then this was the inevitable result.
Even if it hadn't happened today—even if it were tomorrow, the day after, or next week—we would have just sat there slack-jawed until it did.
...My knees buckled... and I sank to the floor.
...Just as Mion... and Rika-chan had done.
...And I, too... could do nothing but shut my eyes tight... and weep...
"...W-What is all this commotion?!@k@vS03/22/141400093. ...What happened in here?!"
Sensei arrived... and her face contorted... at the devastation before her.
The instant Satoko registered the teacher's presence, another drastic transformation overtook her.
...She hastily wiped her face with the curtains... and put on a smile as though nothing had happened.
"Houjou-san...?@k@vS03/22/141400095. What on earth happened here?!"
"...Oh-ho, hohohohohohoho!@k@vS03/04/140400143. I just had a little... quarrel with Rena-san, that's all.@k@vS03/04/140400144. Isn't that right, Rena-san?"
Rena didn't nod in response...@kBut... since that was what Satoko wanted... she gave a feeble nod.
...The rest of the class... couldn't keep up with Satoko's sudden transformation.@k...All they could do... was stand there in stunned silence.
"...Oh-ho-ho... we just got a little carried away with our horseplay... that's all.@k@vS03/04/140400146. Isn't that right, everyone?"
Satoko... would probably talk her way out of this.
...For what?@k...Her heart had been ripped to shreds... what more could she possibly endure... and for what?!
You... just now, you called out for Nii-nii to save you, didn't you...?
But... I'm sorry to say this... Satoshi... has no intention of coming back!!
...Reality isn't so kind that he'll just conveniently appear and rescue you!!
So... you don't need to wait for Satoshi anymore!!@kIt's okay to cry for help... it's okay to run away!!@kWoaaaaaaahhhhhhhhhhhhh!!!
changeReason: Minor stylistic difference in the wording; both old and new correctly convey the primal roar and the preceding dialogue about not needing to wait for Satoshi.

TextENGNew: "...Because we're...... so powerless...!!"
Mion... murmured it.
...Those words that had tormented me time and again... she spoke them.
When those cursed words reached my ears—
...my entire body suddenly... regained its composure.
...Smoothly, as if by magic... the tidal wave of emotion receded.
In that moment... the world's colors inverted!
My field of vision expanded sharply.
My mind became razor-clear, and I felt the space inside my skull expand far beyond the confines of this classroom.
In that moment, my soul had unmistakably risen—no longer contained within the tiny vessel of my head, but hovering slightly above, perceiving everything.
It was... a strange sense of liberation.
...I'd broken free of the cramped shell of my skull... and could stretch my roots in every direction... as far as I wanted.
...A strange sensation I'd never experienced before.@k...A peculiar... exhilaration.
And that honed, crystallized mind... carried me beyond myself!!
My consciousness, now sharper than any blade... instantly judged that spending time being crushed by grief was a waste—and ruthlessly purged those emotions.
...Deleting unnecessary information from my brain.
Purging every useless emotion that would hinder action.
``...........Mion.@k@vS03/01/140100541. ...You go ahead and curse.@k@vS03/01/140100542. Your own powerlessness, for as long as you like.``
changeReason: Old says 'our powerlessness' while JPN 自分の無力さ means 'your own powerlessness' — Keiichi is telling Mion to curse her own powerlessness while he acts, not sharing in it.

TextENGNew: ...If lamenting could undo what had happened, I would lament as long as it took.
...But the fact remained that things had escalated to this point.
What had happened could not be undone.
...Therefore, the highest priority was to halt any further deterioration—to sever the chain of misfortune.
...Until today, I had been thinking with an impossibly narrow perspective.
To save Satoko, the only option was to save Satoko—that was all I'd ever considered.
...I'd refused to acknowledge the most fundamental, most direct, most absolute and singular solution: removing the root cause of Satoko's suffering.
No... I'd actually realized it yesterday.
But for the means, I'd pinned my hopes on some nonsense like 'Oyashiro-sama's curse' rather than considering doing it myself.
I would eliminate Satoko's uncle.
There were plenty of ways to accomplish it.@kThe methods were practically infinite.
Ironically, while almost every plan to rescue Satoko required resources and capital,@kthe vast majority of methods to eliminate that man required almost nothing.
He could be eliminated with the barest minimum of investment.
The barest minimum—that was all he was worth.
That was the measure of that man's life.
Worthless information was being culled from my mind one after another... replaced by knowledge needed solely to carry out the objective.
I redirected every cell in my brain toward a single purpose: the elimination of that man.
The method of killing didn't matter.
Any method, from any era, from any land—I would authorize all of them, so long as certain and swift death lay at the end.
If there was one additional condition, it would be preferable that I not get caught.
His removal was an act designed to restore peace to our lives—mine, our friends', and Satoko's.
If my arrest accompanied his removal, it would be no different from a mutual destruction.
Only he would disappear, and our ordinary days would return.@kThat was the supreme objective.@kThe absolute goal.
Kill him, without fail.
But leave absolutely no evidence.
Two demands on opposite ends of a scale.
The condition of leaving no trace automatically narrowed the available methods of killing.
No witnesses was another absolute requirement.
Fortunately, at the right time of day, Hinamizawa was virtually deserted.
I mentally reconstructed the terrain around Satoko's house.
...Foot traffic patterns by time of day.@kBehavioral algorithms of nearby residents.
Lure him out, or attack directly?@kWhat weapon?@kWhen and where to execute?
Location—it had to be somewhere I could act without leaving a trace.
Timing—on top of that, I had to execute as quickly as possible, for Satoko's sake, not wasting even a fraction of a second.
How utterly trivial.
Thinking about it this way, it struck me how absurdly easy the act of killing really was.
But reason held people back.
The ultimate deterrent against murder was, in the end, nothing more than the fear of being arrested by the police.
If you were out on the open ocean, nothing but horizon in every direction, and you knew for certain no evidence would remain—anyone would push the person they hated overboard.
If it were just that man, it would be almost too easy.
I could walk out of this classroom right now, pick up a metal bat from the schoolyard, and storm his house.
Estimated time required: 25 minutes.
If I was prepared to go down with him, it could be done in a mere 1,500 seconds.
That was how tenuous his continued existence was.
He was so insignificant that a single decision on my part could expel him from this world in just 1,500 seconds.
From the moment I decided, his remaining lifespan was a mere 1,500 seconds.
No... if I sprinted to his house, that lifespan would shrink even further.
But that man was still alive.
He had torn Satoko's body and soul to shreds, and yet he still drew breath at this very moment.
Why?!
Because I was allowing him to live.
I was granting him permission to exist, and that was the only reason he still breathed.
The moment I revoked that permission, he would have to vacate this world within 1,500 seconds.
...You think I'll let you breathe the air of this earth for even 1,500 more seconds?
...That permission is hereby revoked—effective this instant.@kBe grateful to me that you've survived this long.
But in practice, I needed more than 1,500 seconds.
Because this wasn't just about disposing of him—the most important thing was restoring our former days.
...Only his existence would be cleanly erased from this world.@kThe culprit would never be found.
I'd forget that I ever dirtied my hands on the likes of him—as naturally and effortlessly as forgetting to brush my teeth before bed.
To achieve that, I would grant him a special extension beyond 1,500 seconds.
I would graciously permit him to continue his life unchanged until the perfect murder plan was in place.
But Keiichi—@kyou can't waste that time.
Yeah, I know.@kKill him swiftly.@kRemove him completely.
I wouldn't spend unnecessary time devising the plan, either.
...Think—tomorrow is Watanagashi, isn't it?
Last year, on the night of Watanagashi, the aunt had died.
Her skull had been caved in by some maniac, her brains splattered everywhere.
That's right—he was someone who deserved to die on the night of Watanagashi.
Hey, Oyashiro-sama.
You call yourself a guardian deity, yet you didn't protect Satoko.
You're free to curse Satoko's family over the dam issue, but the result of your curse made Satoko—who had nothing to do with the dam—miserable.
...In the name of Keiichi Maebara, I declare you unfit as Hinamizawa's guardian deity.@kYou are no longer needed.@kGo hide in the back of your shrine and sleep through this year.
If you'd just cursed and killed both the uncle and the aunt last year, none of this would have happened!
This year's curse won't come from you.
I will decide.
Not some vague, shadowy cabal of Three Families who may or may not exist—I, myself, will determine the target of this year's curse.
So stay out of my way and watch.@kWatch in silence.@kI will annihilate him completely.@kErase him from the face of the earth.@kHe will vanish without a trace—
Vanish! Disappear! And die!!
Just as you shredded Satoko's heart, I will shred yours!!@kPay for it with your blood!!!
WRAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAGHHHHHHH!!!
"...Keiichi-kun... are you all right...?"
Rena, Mion... and Rika-chan were hovering over me.@k...Had I... been lying on the floor...?
"...You collapsed so suddenly—you startled us.@k@vS03/02/140200164. ...Were you having some kind of nightmare?@k@vS03/02/140200165. ...You were moaning..."
"Shut up.@k@vS03/01/140100544. ...Don't talk to me in that grating voice."
My friends flinched and stepped back.
Rena and the others... exchanged uneasy glances, at a loss for what to say.
"...W-... Was that... your voice just now, Keiichi-kun...?"
Rena swallowed hard... then spoke.
"...Just now, for a second... Keiichi-kun... your eyes were... terrifying."
"...My eyes...?@k@vS03/01/140100548. It was probably just the angle of the light.@k@vS03/01/140100549. ...I just had a bad dream. I'm fine."
"...N-... I'm not mistaken.@k@vS03/03/140300281. ...Those eyes... I've never seen you look like that before."
...What were they going on about?
...They'd all been way too jumpy around me lately.
"...............You are Keiichi Maebara-kun... right?"
What... was Rena saying?
...Who else could I possibly look like?
...That irritated thought must have been written all over my face.
Rena picked up on it and quickly apologized.
"...I-I'm sorry.@k@vS03/02/140200173. ...Don't worry about it!"
